mnemonics.title: RVO Rule of Thumb
mnemonics.content: RVO Happens: Named variables (NRVO), Temporary objects, C++17 mandatory - no copy needed!

mnemonics.title: Reference Safety Check
mnemonics.content: Before returning reference: Ask 'Does this object outlive the function call?' If no, DON'T return reference!

mnemonics.title: Return Type Selection
mnemonics.content: LOCAL: Return by value | MEMBER: Return const ref | OPTIONAL: Return pointer | OWNERSHIP: Return unique_ptr

acronyms.usage: Compiler optimization that constructs return value in caller's memory location, eliminating copy operations.
RVO applied to named local variables (not just temporaries). Optional in most compilers.
Resource management pattern where resource lifetime is tied to object lifetime - used by smart pointers.
An expression that refers to a memory location and can appear on left side of assignment.
A temporary expression that cannot be assigned to; can be moved from.

visualTips.visualization: Draw two boxes: function stack and main stack. Show arrow from function box to main box with 'copy' label crossing the boundary.
Draw single object in memory with two labels pointing to it: one inside function and one in main. Show they refer to same location.
Draw function box with variable being destroyed (X mark), but reference arrow still pointing to that spot in now-empty memory.
Draw single box in main stack. Show compiler skipping function's local variable creation and building directly in that box.

analogies.analogy: Returning by value is like photocopying a document and handing it over. Returning by reference is like giving someone the original document to use.
It's like writing down a friend's temporary address while they're staying somewhere for a week - after they leave, your address points to nowhere.
It's like the factory shipping a product directly to the customer instead of shipping to a warehouse first - saves an extra handling step.
It's like moving to a new house - instead of copying everything, you pack up and transfer ownership. The old place becomes empty.
